items.title: Enhance SSR with Code-Split CSS Injection in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of unstyled content during server-side rendering by injecting route-specific code-split CSS directly into the HTML. Key changes include modifications to `server.mjs` and `entry-server.ts` to utilize the SSR manifest, ensuring that all necessary stylesheets are loaded before the first paint. As a result, users will experience a smoother and more visually consistent loading process.

items.title: Fix empty tooltips in Stage badges for smoother hydration in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of empty tooltips causing hydration mismatches in the Stage badges. By moving the conditional rendering to the `<el-tooltip>` component, we ensure that tooltips only render when their corresponding buttons are present. This change eliminates the `ElOnlyChild` warnings while maintaining the visual output for production APIs.

items.title: Fix FontAwesome SSR Rendering Issues in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a server-side rendering (SSR) shim for FontAwesome icons, resolving hydration mismatches that caused icons to render incorrectly. The shim ensures that string and array icons are properly converted to their SVG definitions on the server, eliminating hydration warnings and improving SEO for crawlers. All affected components now import the FontAwesomeIcon from the new shim, maintaining client-side integrity.

items.title: Enhance SSR Data Transfer for Seamless Hydration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a per-request data transfer mechanism for server-side rendered (SSR) pages, eliminating the need for clients to refetch data on mount. Key changes include the introduction of new utility functions for SSR data management and modifications to multiple page components to ensure they utilize this data effectively. The result is a smoother user experience with reduced hydration mismatches and improved performance.

items.title: Revamp SSR Strategy: Say Goodbye to SSG in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update completely removes the build-time SSG, ensuring that users receive a dynamic SPA experience by default, while crawlers benefit from live SSR. Key changes include modifications to the Dockerfile and nginx configuration, streamlining the build process and enhancing routing for both human users and bots. The removal of dead SSG code simplifies the codebase and improves performance.

items.title: Enhance Branding with Richer Content and Transitions in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a vibrant colored AceData logo without a chip, addressing previous feedback for a more visually appealing design. Key enhancements include varied transitions for smoother animations, single-logo de-duplication, and richer content with real endpoint and pricing cards. Additionally, results are now captioned with the actual prompts that generated them, improving clarity and engagement.

items.title: Dynamic Currency Formatting for Model Prices in Frontend Web 💰
items.summary: This update replaces hardcoded USD pricing on the models list and detail pages with dynamic currency formatting based on user selection. The `getPrice()` function now handles conversions, ensuring that users see prices in their chosen currency with the correct symbols and precision. All related components have been updated accordingly, and the build is green.

items.title: Streamlined SSR Proxy Configuration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the SSR proxy configuration by replacing the hardcoded DNS resolver with a literal proxy_pass to the service FQDN. This change eliminates reliance on a specific IP address, improving stability and reducing fragility. The deployment scripts have been updated to ensure the SSR service is applied before the frontend, allowing for seamless resolution at startup.

items.title: Enhance AceData Branding with Real Logo and Captions in Platform Studio 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces the genuine AceData logo on every frame, ensuring brand consistency across all visuals. Additionally, a frosted-glass caption pill has been implemented to enhance the clarity and uniformity of captions, replacing the previous gpt-rendered versions. These changes improve the overall quality and professionalism of the marketing shorts produced.

items.title: Enhance SSR Proxy Functionality in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses issues with the SSR proxy, specifically resolving DNS 500 errors and routing 404s. Key modifications include updates to the nginx configuration and deployment settings, ensuring that the SSR service operates smoothly with improved routing performance. Live verification confirms all route types are now returning a 200 status with enhanced response times.

items.title: Revamped Brand-Video Factory with Zero PIL in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a complete refactor of the brand-video factory, eliminating all PIL hand-compositing. Every visual is now generated by gpt-image-2, allowing for seamless integration of real logos, captions, and QR codes directly into the frames. The new approach enhances efficiency and quality, ensuring all outputs are produced natively without reliance on PIL.

items.title: Exciting New AI Model Directory in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The top navigation now features a new 'Models' section, positioned right after 'Services', providing direct access to the AI model directory at '/models'. The 'Roadmap' link has been removed, and the models directory is now publicly accessible, showcasing 154 models with detailed pages and pricing. Additionally, internationalization support has been updated across all 12 locales.

items.title: Introducing True SSR Foundation in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR lays the groundwork for a true server-side rendering (SSR) migration, replacing the previous static site generation with live per-request rendering. Key files added include the SSR server, client entry, and necessary deployment configurations, ensuring fresh data is served on each request. Local tests confirm that the rendering process is efficient, returning live HTML and JSON-LD within 200-900ms.

items.title: Fix iOS Login White Screen Issue in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the white screen issue that occurs for logged-out users in the Nexior iOS app. The problem was caused by a 301 redirect from the login URL that downgraded the scheme to HTTP, which is blocked by iOS App Transport Security. By ensuring the login URL includes a trailing slash, the app now successfully loads the login iframe without errors.

items.title: Enhanced Logo Engine and Diversity Features in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces v13 of the brand-video factory, addressing key issues from v12. Provider logos now appear contextually matched to the narration, resolving the previous complaint about missing logos. Additionally, a new diversity feature ensures each run generates fresh, non-repeating outputs, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Revamp Top-Up Tiers with Stylish Cards in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: The top-up selector on the models page has been redesigned to enhance user experience. Tiers now display as wrapped bordered buttons with corner discount badges, eliminating horizontal scrolling and ensuring no content is cut off. A new shared Recharge component has been introduced for consistency across the list and detail pages, and the layout has been widened to accommodate more tiers.
